        pm.get_container(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container).owned_procs.wf(),
        pm.get_container(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container).children.wf(),
{
}

name: get_proc_ensures_container_dom_from_wf
code:
proof fn phi_3_get_proc_ensures_container_dom_from_wf(pm: ProcessManager, p_ptr: ProcPtr)
    requires
        pm.proc_perms_wf(),
        pm.process_fields_wf(),
        pm.proc_dom().contains(p_ptr),
        pm.wf(),
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.get_proc(p_ptr).owning_container),
{
}
reason: `get_proc`'s external_body ensures `self.wf() ==> self.container_dom().contains(ret.owning_container)` — this cross-subsystem containment guarantee is asserted by the accessor rather than by a dedicated relational invariant

name: thread_inv_external_body_unchecked
code:
proof fn phi_4_thread_inv_external_body_unchecked(pm: ProcessManager, t_ptr: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.thread_dom().contains(t_ptr),
    ensures
        pm.proc_dom().contains(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_proc),
        pm.get_proc(pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_proc).owning_container == pm.get_thread(t_ptr).owning_container,
{
}
reason: `thread_inv` is `external_body` — the thread-to-process ownership consistency (proc contains thread, proc's container matches thread's container) is axiomatically assumed without proof

name: endpoint_inv_queue_threads_in_dom
code:
proof fn phi_5_endpoint_inv_queue_threads_in_dom(pm: ProcessManager, e_ptr: EndpointPtr, i: int)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.endpoint_dom().contains(e_ptr),
        0 <= i < pm.get_endpoint(e_ptr).queue.len(),
    ensures
        pm.thread_dom().contains(pm.get_endpoint(e_ptr).queue@[i]),
        pm.get_thread(pm.get_endpoint(e_ptr).queue@[i]).state == ThreadState::BLOCKED,
{
    pm.endpoint_inv();
}
reason: `endpoint_inv` is `external_body` — the guarantee that all threads in an endpoint queue are valid and BLOCKED is trusted without proof, which could mask invariant violations during concurrent queue manipulation
